catalysts_general: Director Kenneth Courtis accumulated $30M+ in open-market purchases over 6 months, but met coal faces secular demand questions and experts flagged conflicting reasons (Tier 1: SEC Form 4)

Discovery: Director Kenneth Courtis has accumulated over $30M in open-market purchases over 6 months (56+ transactions), including ~$6.1M in March alone (15K shares on Mar 10 at $182, 25K shares on Mar 11, 8K shares on Mar 20) — extraordinary persistent conviction buying in this met coal producer
growth_picks.analyst_take: Director accumulated $30M+ in purchases but met coal is cyclical and conflicting expert signals lower conviction

catalysts_general: Director bought $136.5M — one of largest insider purchases of 2026, but Korea growth slowing and Farfetch luxury integration challenging (Tier 1: SEC Form 4)

catalysts_general: Up 72% YTD and 280% over past year on drone/autonomous defense systems demand, Pentagon spending $1B+ on drones — but rally is priced in (Tier 5: financial aggregators)

Discovery: Iran war driving surging demand for affordable autonomous combat drones and hypersonics — Valkyrie drone achieved two programs of record, Marines and another classified; revenue guided up 21% to $1.6-1.7B in 2026; supplies microwave electronics for Iron Dome, Arrow, and classified missile programs seeing intensified use in Operation Epic Fury.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Defense drone leader but up 280% in past year — valuation reflects the thesis, limited margin of safety

catalysts_general: Data center power and cooling demand surging from AI buildout, but stock has significantly re-rated already (Tier 5: financial aggregators)

Discovery: Critical AI data center power and liquid cooling supplier with record $15B backlog providing 12-18 month revenue visibility, 2026 guidance of $13.3-13.7B revenue (28% growth) with 43% earnings growth and $2.3B FCF, co-developing 800V DC power architecture with Nvidia for Rubin Ultra 2027 rollout.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Data center power/cooling beneficiary but has already rallied significantly and valuation reflects AI demand

catalysts_general: Fabrinet optical components riding AI networking demand, but customer concentration and premium valuation (Tier 5: financial aggregators)

growth_picks.analyst_take: Optical interconnect beneficiary of AI networking buildout but concentration risk and premium valuation limit upside

catalysts_general: Defense budget $1.01T beneficiary, space/missile systems demand rising, but moderate growth rates for large defense prime (Tier 5: financial aggregators)

Discovery: Awarded $400M contract in Feb 2026 to build THAAD rocket boost motors and control systems as Pentagon quadruples THAAD interceptor production from 96 to 400/year; agreed to increase capex 38% and pause buybacks to meet Iran war munitions demand; solid rocket motor production in Alabama directly feeds conflict-critical missile defense systems.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Defense prime benefiting from Iran conflict spending but moderate growth and fair valuation limit near-term upside

catalysts_general: Tanker rates spiking on Strait of Hormuz disruption, but highly cyclical and rates will normalize when geopolitical tensions ease (Tier 5: financial aggregators)

Discovery: Strait of Hormuz closure forcing global oil rerouting around Cape of Good Hope, nearly doubling ton-miles and creating severe VLCC shortage; Q1 2026 contracted spot TCE of $107,100/day (up from $74,200 in Q4 2025); VLCC day rates hit all-time high of $423,736; war risk premiums surged 300% — Frontline's fleet of 82 vessels positioned as direct beneficiary of sustained rerouting.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Tanker rates elevated on Hormuz disruption but cyclical nature and potential conflict resolution create downside risk
